name: iWish
subtitle: Tcl/Tk (AndroWish) on iOS
description: AltStore source for iWish — AndroWish's batteries-included, SDL2-rendered Tcl/Tk wish, ported to iOS/iPadOS. Add this source in AltStore/SideStore to install and auto-update iWish.

apps.name: iWish
apps.subtitle: Batteries-included Tcl/Tk with SDL2
apps.localizedDescription:
A real Tcl 8.6 interpreter and Tk 8.6 widget set on an iPad or iPhone, rendered through AndroWish's SdlTk (X11-on-SDL2) with AGG + FreeType — no WebView, no UIKit widget bridge. ~114 bundled packages (64 native arm64 dylibs incl. tkimg, tls, TclCurl, sqlite3, itcl, TkBLT, Tix, vectcl), a File > Demos menu, borg (device bridge) and ble (CoreBluetooth) shims, and a LightBlue-style BLE debugger.

Alpha software — expect sharp edges. Not on the App Store (it's an interpreter that loads compiled extensions). See the project page for details.

versions.localizedDescription:
**iWish 0.2.1-alpha** — Tcl/Tk (AndroWish `undroidwish`) on iOS/iPadOS, `arm64-apple-ios`.

### What changed since 0.2-alpha
This is a **packaging fix so AltStore / SideStore can install iWish.** No feature or code changes — functionally identical to 0.2-alpha.

Previously, AltStore/SideStore installs failed at the last step with:

> Failed to verify code signature … `0xe8008001` (An unknown error has occurred.)

**Cause:** iWish ships ~63 native Tcl/Tk extension `.dylib`s. They used to live under `lib-batteries/…`. AltStore (via `ldid`) re-signs the app executable and everything in **`Frameworks/`**, but *not* dylibs in other folders — so those kept their original signature and iOS rejected the bundle.

**Fix:** all native `.dylib`s now live in **`iWish.app/Frameworks/`**, and the Tcl `load` paths were updated accordingly. AltStore/SideStore now re-sign every binary consistently, so the install completes. Verified end-to-end (all bundled packages load; an `ldid` resign leaves zero unsigned Mach-O).

If a previous attempt failed: refresh the iWish source in AltStore/SideStore (pull to refresh) and install/update again.

### Installing
`iWish.ipa` is a **development build and is UNSIGNED** — Sideloadly / AltStore / SideStore re-sign it with your own Apple ID (free = 7-day, paid = 1 year). See **[INSTALL.md](https://github.com/johnbuckman/iwish/blob/main/INSTALL.md)**.

> Not on the App Store (it's an interpreter that `dlopen`s C extensions). Alpha — expect sharp edges.

**iWish 0.2-alpha** — Tcl/Tk (AndroWish `undroidwish`) on iOS/iPadOS, `arm64-apple-ios`.

A real Tcl 8.6 interpreter + Tk 8.6 widget set on an iPad/iPhone, rendered through AndroWish's SdlTk (X11-on-SDL2) with AGG + FreeType — no WebView, no UIKit widget bridge.

### What's new since 0.1-alpha
- **Full batteries-included build** — ~114 bundled packages, 64 native `arm64-apple-ios` dylibs (tkimg, tls/LibreSSL, TclCurl, sqlite3, itcl, itk, thread, tdom, Tktable, tktreectrl, zint, Img, tkpath, tkvnc, BLT 2.4, **TkBLT**, **Tix**, **vectcl**, tksvg, …).
- **File ▸ Demos menu** in the console with bundled demo apps, including four iWish-specific ones: `bltgraph` (TkBLT plotting), `bledemo` (LightBlue-style BLE debugger), `borgdemo` (the `borg` iOS bridge), `paint`.
- **`borg`** (device bridge) and **`ble`** (CoreBluetooth) iOS shims.
- New docs: [`INSTALL.md`](https://github.com/johnbuckman/iwish/blob/main/INSTALL.md) (Sideloadly / AltStore / from source / EU notarized) and [`AGENTS.md`](https://github.com/johnbuckman/iwish/blob/main/AGENTS.md).

### Installing
`iWish.ipa` is a **development build and is UNSIGNED** — you must re-sign it with your own Apple ID before it will launch. Easiest paths:
- **Sideloadly** or **AltStore** — re-sign automatically (free Apple ID = 7-day expiry; paid = 1 year).
- **EU devices** — a notarized web/marketplace install has no expiry and needs no computer.

Full step-by-step + a Sideloadly-vs-AltStore comparison: see **[INSTALL.md](https://github.com/johnbuckman/iwish/blob/main/INSTALL.md)**.

> Not on the App Store (and can't be — it's an interpreter that `dlopen`s C extensions). Alpha: expect sharp edges. See [BUGS.md](https://github.com/johnbuckman/iwish/blob/main/BUGS.md).

privacy.name: BluetoothAlways
privacy.usageDescription: iWish's ble command and BLE debugger use Bluetooth to scan for and connect to nearby devices.

privacy.name: BluetoothPeripheral
privacy.usageDescription: iWish's ble command and BLE debugger use Bluetooth to communicate with nearby devices.
